info.title: CureMD FHIR API (unofficial OpenAPI description)
info.summary: Read-only FHIR R4 / US Core 3.1.1 API for the CureMD EHR.
info.description:
UNOFFICIAL OpenAPI 3.1 description of CureMD's FHIR API, derived from CureMD's public
developer documentation (https://www.curemd.com/developer/fhir-apis.pdf). Not produced,
sponsored, or endorsed by CureMD. Verify against CureMD's own documentation before relying on it.

Key facts encoded here:
- FHIR Release 4, US Core 3.1.1, USCDI v1.
- READ / SEARCH ONLY — no create/update/delete operations exist.
- Authorization: SMART App Launch STU2 (OAuth 2.0 + OpenID Connect). PKCE (S256) is MANDATORY
  for all user-facing flows; OpenAPI's oauth2 scheme does not model PKCE explicitly.
- The base URL is PER-TENANT, issued by CureMD at registration; set the `baseUrl` server variable.
- The authorization and token URLs are discovered at runtime via GET {baseUrl}/.well-known/smart-configuration;
  the values in the oauth2 flow below are placeholders.
- Rate limit: 20 requests/minute per client (HTTP 429 over the limit).
- Cost: patient-facing access to a patient's own data is free; clinician/backend access requires a
  signed business agreement with the practice and additional cost.
- Full FHIR R4 resource schemas are not reproduced; responses are typed as generic FHIR resources/Bundles.

servers.description: Per-tenant CureMD FHIR base URL, issued at app registration.

baseUrl.description: Replace with the base URL CureMD provisions for your practice/tenant.

get.description: Returns the server's FHIR CapabilityStatement — the canonical, machine-readable description of what this CureMD tenant supports.
Authorization-server metadata. Extract authorization_endpoint, token_endpoint, and jwks_uri here before starting the SMART App Launch flow.
Asynchronous FHIR Bulk Data export for all patients in scope. Send `Prefer: respond-async`; the response returns a content-location to poll. Subject to the read-only and rate-limit constraints.

202.description: Accepted — poll the URL in the Content-Location header for export status and output files.

smartOnFhir.description: SMART App Launch STU2. PKCE (code_challenge_method=S256) is mandatory. authorizationUrl/tokenUrl below are placeholders — discover the real values via GET {baseUrl}/.well-known/smart-configuration.

scopes.launch: Permission to obtain launch context when launched from CureMD (EHR launch).
scopes.openid: OpenID Connect — identity of the authorizing user.
scopes.fhirUser: Retrieve the FHIR resource representing the current user.
scopes.offline_access: Request a refresh token for long-lived access.
scopes.patient/Patient.rs: Read and search Patient resources for the in-context patient.
scopes.patient/AllergyIntolerance.rs: Read and search AllergyIntolerance resources for the in-context patient.

FhirResource.description: A FHIR R4 resource (US Core 3.1.1 profile). Schema is intentionally generic; see the FHIR R4 / US Core specs for full element definitions.

Forbidden.description: Token lacks the required scope, or access is not permitted for this tenant/patient.

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded (20 requests/minute per client). Back off and retry.
